[PUBLISHED DATE]: Dec 30, 2013
[TITLE]: The "Stanbridge White" Duck Breed Thread | BackYard Chickens - Learn How to Raise Chickens
[METADATA/INTRO]: - Dec 30, 2013 - 20...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: INTRODUCTION TO THE BREED 
 The Stanbridge White duck is a breed that was believed to be extinct until 2007, when the president of the rare poultry society identified the breed in Gloucestershire. The birds had been maintained by William Osbourne, an experienced poultry keeper who by 2009 maintained fourteen Stanbridge ducks and four drakes in three breeding pens. 
 William purchased his original birds from an elderly man in the village of Aylburton in Gloucestershire, the same village that a known poultry photographer (Mr Arthur Rice) had lived and kept the Stanbridge. The elderly man did not know what they were and only regarded them as “Farmyard ducks” but he did state they “laid like stink”. I find this so cute and also a bit astonishing! The Marsh Daisy chicken was also "found" this way.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2011-02-04
[TITLE]: Duck, Duck, Chickens | river of love urban farmstead
[METADATA/INTRO]: Ducks. We’ve had our first chickens for only a few months, and we’re madly in love with them. Aren’t we still adjusting to chickens? At first I just couldn’t wrap my mind around the idea of keeping ducks, but I...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Ducks. We’ve had our first chickens for only a few months, and we’re madly in love with them. Aren’t we still adjusting to chickens? At first I just couldn’t wrap my mind around the idea of keeping ducks, but I knew about the benefits. Like chickens, they lay eggs, add manure for rich compost, and they are excellent bug control for gardens. Most of all, they’re comical… we can never have enough comedy around here! They’re able to house with the chickens and are totally compatible. I was told by a duck keeper that they tend to have even better vision than chickens, and can spot hawks overhead more quickly, which alerts the chickens. Protectors? Maybe. Sorin was the first person who mentioned ducks, and I had no desire at the time. Despite that, I spent a few weeks toying with the idea, discussing the prospect with Daniel. We dropped the idea, and re-visited it several times.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Fawn and White Runner Duck | Ornamental Breed
[METADATA/INTRO]: Why is their appearance considered to be unusual? They stand almost upright, compared to other duck breeds that are positioned low to the ground. They have many nicknames, including "penguin" or "bowling pin." What is the history of this breed?...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: What do Fawn and White runner ducklings look like? They have yellow downy feathers. As they develop their adult feathers, their signature coloration will start to come in. 
 What is this breed prized for? Many growers enjoy their fun personalities and active, inquisitive nature. They are also great for gardeners; they help keep weed, insect, and slug populations down. 
 Are Runners recognized by the American Standard of Perfection (APA)? Yes, they were recognized by the Standard of Perfection in 1898, categorized in the "light" class. 
 What is the best kind of environment for these ducks? They love to forage and explore, so raising them in a free-range environment with lots of space would be best.
